name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium
tagline: A climate-controlled cathedral with a camera-shutter roof, hosting eight matches that climax in a July 15 World Cup semifinal.

why.title: The Pinwheel Roof
why.text: Eight translucent triangular panels slide open like a camera aperture in about eight minutes. Nothing else in sports looks like it.

why.title: Halo Board
why.text: A 360° video ring wraps the entire roof rim, the size of three NFL jumbotrons. Every seat sees everything.

why.title: Semifinal Stage
why.text: One of only two semifinals worldwide, feeding the final at MetLife. Atlanta is the last stop before the throne.

why.title: Soccer City
why.text: Atlanta United routinely packs 70,000-plus and shatters MLS attendance records. This town shows up loud.

gettingThere: The stadium sits right in downtown Atlanta, a mile from Centennial Olympic Park. Skip the car — MARTA rail drops you at the doorstep.
transit: MARTA rail is the move: GWCC/CNN Center station is essentially at the gates, Vine City is a 10-min walk and dodges traffic
From Hartsfield-Jackson airport (ATL), take the Red/Gold line to Five Points and transfer to Blue/Green — about $2.50 each way
Official GWCC parking decks and lots exist but expect surge pricing and gridlock on match days; reserve ahead if you must drive
Rideshare zones are on Northside Dr and Broad St, but post-match surge waits are brutal — MARTA clears out faster
matchday: The official FIFA Fan Festival takes over Centennial Olympic Park downtown — free entry, a 40-ft screen, concerts, and food vendors across 18 days from June 11. The Home Depot Backyard, an 11-acre green west of the stadium, is the tailgate hub.
food: Fan First pricing is the real headline: $2 refillable sodas, $5 beers, all whole-dollar with tax baked in — cheapest in U.S. sports
Best of Atlanta stands bring Chick-fil-A and The Varsity inside at street prices
670+ points of sale, fully cashless, so lines move fast
Outside the gates, hit West Midtown and the BeltLine's Ponce City Market for breweries and nightlife
tips: Take MARTA, not a car — Vine City or GWCC stations beat parking and rideshare surge every time
Eat and drink inside; the whole-dollar Fan First menu is unbeatable, no need to overspend outside
Arrive early for World Cup security and bring a clear bag — the bag policy is strict, or go bag-free
Aim for the lower-bowl supporters' ends behind the goals for the loudest atmosphere
The roof stays closed and AC is on, so dress for indoor comfort — but pack a poncho for the muggy walk to transit
Hit the free Fan Festival at Centennial Park even without a ticket; pre-register and walk over from downtown
weather: June-July Atlanta is hot and humid with highs near 88°F and pop-up afternoon storms, but the roof stays closed and the stadium is air-conditioned — pack a packable poncho for the walk and dress light.
lore: FIFA strips the Mercedes-Benz name during the tournament, branding it 'Atlanta Stadium' — but the iconic roof star stays put
Built for ~$1.6 billion and opened in 2017, it was the first LEED Platinum pro sports stadium in the U.S.
Hosted Super Bowl LIII, two College Football Playoff title games, and the 2024 Copa América opener
Atlanta United won the 2018 MLS Cup here in front of 73,019 — a championship roar this city won't forget
